source_pr.title: executor: Fix tidb crash on index merge reader
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #40877

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?
Let both `pickAndExecTask` and `handlePickAndExecTaskPanic` take the address of `task` as its argument, so once `*task` is assigned in `pickAndExecTask`,  `handlePickAndExecTaskPanic` will get a non nil value.
### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: IndexMerge table-scan worker could crash in the panic-recovery path: the panic handler captured a nil task pointer and could not report the error via task.doneCh.
failure_signature: panic in IndexMergeReaderExecutor indexMergeTableWorker / nil task in handlePickAndExecTaskPanic (cannot send error to task.doneCh)
root_cause_explanation: The crash was caused by a bug in async worker recovery: `pickAndExecTask` assigns the `task` pointer after it receives a work item, but the panic handler previously received `task` as a value argument (Golang pass-by-value). If a panic occurred, the handler could still see a nil task and crash/lose the chance to send the panic error to `task.doneCh`. This PR fixes it by passing the address of `task` into both `pickAndExecTask` and `handlePickAndExecTaskPanic` (so the assignment is visible) and guarding against nil before sending the error, with a failpoint-based regression test.
fix_pattern: Pass &task (pointer-to-pointer) into pickAndExecTask and the panic handler so the assigned task is visible during recovery; guard nil before sending to doneCh; add failpoint regression test.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select /*+ use_index_merge(t1, primary, t1a) */ * from t1 where id < 2 or a > 4 order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1 1 1 1", (executor/index_merge_reader_test.go)
